item.title: Re: wow dude, you sure post quick.
item.content:encoded:
In the second footnote:

 previous lives in an attempt to reconcile with whatever discourse that has prevented them from moving on.

should probably read 
 previous lives in an attempt to reconcile with whatever discord that has prevented them from moving on.

item.title: there's something strange in the neighborhood...
item.content:encoded: Okay, interesting premise, I'll give you that at least. TBH, I don't know why there aren't more skips dealing with ghosts and specters given the nature of the Foundation. That said, there's just something about the execution of that promise that's bugging me. I think I'll abstain from voting either way until I figure out what that is (I can't vary fairly downvote without at least giving criticism on why)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, you though that was me who did all that?

I believe this was supposed to say "thought."
Otherwise, quite interesting SCP. Hesitant no-vote, as I think the descriptions are great but the premise is altogether not my taste.

item.title: Tags?
item.content:encoded: Is anyone planning to add any tags to this anytime soon?

item.title: Re: wow dude, you sure post quick.
item.content:encoded: i really like the idea that the D-class that died are causing trouble for the foundation +1

item.title: wow dude, you sure post quick.
item.content:encoded:
I'm proud of the hexadecimal designation, even if it makes it slightly harder to say out loud; we can assume by 2039 that the Foundation has gone through thousands of D-Class, and the need for an information-dense and human-readable representation of that would drive schemes such as this. Octal or a UUID-based system would work even better. There's a story to be written in a D-Class having a hash collision with another, deceased D-Class and being written up as an anomaly (much to their benefit).

- Jekeled, About 3333

Thanks to Prometheus on discord for reading this and checking for any problems.

Source comes from here
